name: Mariarta
description: An older, canny courtesan of the Promise of Heaven with 'the calm intelligence of someone who can survive almost any situation involving men.' Sent up to entertain the Baron's soldiers, she brings back the hard intelligence on his guarded quarters -- Randall's most valuable inside source on the Dragon Knights' manor.
narrative: Mariarta (the novelization's Moriata) is one of the older women of the Promise of Heaven, calm and shrewd, long practiced at reading men and outlasting trouble. When the Baron's soldiers sent for company, she was among the girls who went up to the fortified manor -- and she came back with exactly what Randall needed: the Baron never left his quarters, no one was permitted near them, the room sat second-storey on the eastern side, and the guards were too disciplined to trifle with. She'll keep gathering for him, and ask only that he find something in it for the girls. No fighter -- her weapons are patience, charm, and a clear eye.

extras.role: An older, shrewd working woman of the Promise of Heaven -- 'the calm intelligence of someone who can survive almost any situation involving men.' She was among the girls sent up to the Baron's manor to entertain his soldiers, and brings back the crucial intelligence: the Baron keeps to his quarters, no one is allowed near, the room is second-storey on the eastern side, the guards are disciplined. Randall's most valuable inside source on Hanzo's household.
extras.relationship: Works alongside [[asphodel]] at the Promise of Heaven; affectionate, lifelong-acquaintance ease with [[randall]].
extras.correction_note: Recast from an earlier mistaken 'canal-runner / smuggler' Rogue kit. Per the novelization 'Moriata' is an older courtesan at the Promise of Heaven who went to the Baron's manor and gathered intel -- a social informant, not a thief.
extras.source: Novelization (And then the Dragons Came, ch. 13) -- 'Moriata' (= Mariarta), the older girl who went up to the manor.
